name: understand-codebase
summary: Build a repo fingerprint before touching code — entry points, architecture, invariants, conventions.
when_to_use: First session on an unfamiliar repo. Before any non-trivial change to an unfamiliar subsystem.

phases.name: Orient
steps: Read README, CLAUDE.md, and docs/architecture if present.
List top-level directories with one-line descriptions of what each holds.
Identify the package manager, build tool, test runner from lockfile + config files.

phases.name: Entry points
steps: Find all entry points: main/index files, CLI binaries, HTTP handlers, background workers.
For each: trace the first level of imports — what does it immediately depend on?

phases.name: Invariants
steps: What must always be true in this codebase? Data integrity, auth required, schema versions.
Grep for assertions, invariant comments, type guards.

phases.name: Conventions
steps: Naming patterns (camelCase, snake_case, PascalCase per file type).
Error-handling style (exceptions, Result types, error codes).
Test co-location (alongside source or separate dir?).

phases.name: Produce fingerprint
steps: Write a 1-page summary: what this repo is, how it's structured, what matters, what to avoid.
Save to engram with topic_key cc/repo/{slug}/fingerprint.

exit_criteria: You can answer: what does this repo do, what are its invariants, what conventions will I follow, what's off-limits?
anti_patterns: Skipping this pack on unfamiliar repos.
Reading one file and assuming the rest follows.
Not writing the fingerprint down — context dies at session end.
